mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.0 percent, it signals a labor market inflection that reduces precautionary savings and boosts consumer spending, driving immediate positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized real-economy expansion, which compresses credit spreads and elevates corporate earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from periods where only one series rises.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of negative real GDP growth, a sequential rise in retail sales indicates a bottoming in aggregate demand, which historically precedes a 60-day equity rally as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain negative or flat in over 60% of triggered instances, or RSAFS growth shows no correlation with subsequent earnings revision trends.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, forcing higher discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances following a CPI spike above 0.5%, or the signal fails to predict volatility expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates that employment gains are translating into actual consumption demand, which validates corporate revenue forecasts and sustains positive S&P 500 momentum over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from periods with payroll growth alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trajectory in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost pressures and supports margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO trends upward, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market recovery regime, monthly housing starts exceeding 500 thousand units signal a reversal in the residential investment drag, which rapidly improves consumer confidence and drives immediate positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in over 60% of instances where HOUST crosses above 500, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing.
